title: Naj-Dev Portfolio Website
description: A modern portfolio website showcasing my projects, skills, and experience, built using Next.js and other cutting-edge technologies.
achievements: Designed a visually appealing and responsive user interface.
Implemented a modular structure for scalability and reusability.
Showcased a dynamic project list with support for embedded videos and images.
additionalNotes: Future plans include integrating the T3 stack for backend support, user interactions, and advanced animations leveraging server-side rendering for enhanced performance.

title: JPMorgan Chase — Mobile Development Intern
description: Working on the Chase Android app — used by 70M+ customers — as part of the Software Engineering Program's mobile track, self-teaching Jetpack Compose and MVVM to contribute from day one.
achievements: Self-taught Jetpack Compose and MVVM patterns to accelerate onboarding and contribute to the Chase Android app used by 70M+ users.
Collaborated with developers to refactor existing merchandising tile logic and adapt Android View templates for a new tile design, improving customer interaction by 15%.
Led the transition of the team's package to Jetpack Compose by rewriting merchandising tiles and their bindings, pioneering Compose adoption and enabling more flexible layouts for future features.
additionalNotes: Continuing full-time on this team after graduating from OSU in May 2026.

title: JPMC Global Hackathon — Internal Mobility Assistant (1st Place, Columbus)
description: Built an internal mobility assistant to help JPMorgan Chase employees discover and transition into better-aligned roles, winning 1st place out of 64 teams in the Columbus division of JPMC's 10th Annual Global Hackathon.
achievements: Won 1st place out of 64 teams at the Columbus division of JPMorgan Chase's 10th Annual Global Hackathon.

Built a backend system using NLP techniques and Python's Counter to compare user resumes against job descriptions, scoring overlap to surface best-fit internal roles — designed for future LLM integration.
Collaborated across frontend, backend, and agentic AI teams to deliver a chatbot-driven platform with role-matching logic and automated outreach workflows.
additionalNotes: Built during JPMC's 10th Annual Global Hackathon. The Columbus division had 64 competing teams.

title: JPMorgan Chase — Full Stack Web Development Intern
description: Modernized an internal financial tool's UI for 1000+ global legal entities, transitioning from a legacy JSP application to a responsive React microfrontend — reducing task time by up to 50% and targeting 99% uptime.
achievements: Pioneered the modernization of an internal financial tool's UI, transitioning from an outdated JSP application to a responsive React frontend designed to reduce task time by up to 50% for 1000+ global legal entities.
Worked independently on the UI/UX redesign in Columbus while coordinating closely with an international team of developers, designers, and managers using Jira.
Modularized the new frontend using a microfrontend architecture and existing APIs, enabling partial integration with the legacy system and comprehensive testing with Jest and Webpack.
additionalNotes: Strong focus on independent development, cross-timezone communication, and unit testing discipline.

title: Breakout Simulation Labs
description: A multi-part lab series for creating and enhancing a Breakout game simulation, culminating in advanced skills like register management, assembly programming, and low-level memory operations.
achievements: Built a foundational game simulation in Lab 2 with single ball, paddle, and block mechanics.
Extended functionality in Lab 3 to support multiple objects, scoring, and linked list integration.
Developed custom linked list functions in Lab 4, including `insert`, `deleteSome`, `iterate`, and `sort`.
Implemented advanced memory management techniques to handle allocation failures gracefully.
Enhanced game mechanics with paddle deflection, bonus modes, and optimized output.
Gained expertise in assembly programming with register allocation, stack alignment, and efficient array operations from Lab 5.
Developed assembly functions to analyze audio data, track min/max values, and convert PCM to decibels.
additionalNotes: Developed progressively across Labs 2-5, introducing increasing complexity and refining both high-level and low-level programming skills.

title: Jump The Broom Books Website
description: A responsive website for Jump The Broom Books, showcasing its offerings and providing an online presence.
achievements: Created a user-friendly website to enhance visibility and engagement.
Ensured responsive design across devices.
Delivered a scalable solution using modern web technologies.
additionalNotes: This project highlights the ability to craft modern, responsive websites for specialized use cases.
imageDescription: Jump The Broom Books Website Screenshot

title: Next.js Lightweight CMS
description: A lightweight CMS built with Next.js for dynamic content management, designed for portfolio projects and small-scale websites.
achievements: Integrated rich-text editing and Markdown support for posts.
Designed a modular architecture to support scalability across different applications.
Planned for dynamic content management via a headless CMS setup.
additionalNotes: This project will serve as the foundation for several portfolio pieces, including a new iteration of M.C. Jeter's site.

title: AI-Powered Playlist Generator
description: A machine learning project designed to analyze Last.fm data and generate 20-song playlists based on user preferences and song similarities.
achievements: Developed a pipeline to fetch and process user listening data from the Last.fm API.
Planned for AI-based song similarity analysis using OpenAI models.
Designed an intuitive interface for generating and exporting playlists.
additionalNotes: This project combines music analysis with machine learning to provide personalized playlist recommendations.

title: ECE 2360 Final Project
description: Temperature-activated lamp with RGB LED, LCD, and buzzer functionality.
achievements: Combined sensors and microcontroller programming to create a cohesive hardware project
Resolved hardware and software troubleshooting challenges
additionalNotes: Learned new hardware components and applied GitHub for collaboration.

title: CTRL-R — JPMC Code for Good (Challenge Winner, Pyramid CDC)
description: A digital literacy platform built in 24 hours for Pyramid CDC, a Columbus non-profit improving digital access in underserved Ohio communities. Won the Pyramid CDC challenge at the JPMorgan Chase Code for Good Hackathon.
achievements: Awarded challenge winner for Pyramid CDC at the JPMorgan Chase Code for Good Hackathon, competing to improve digital literacy for 1000+ Ohio residents.
Developed personalized course paths with gamification mechanics to boost engagement and retention.
Specialized in configuring the Firebase store and authentication, integrating it into the full codebase.
Collaborated with a cross-functional team to ship a fully functional, user-friendly platform in 24 hours.
additionalNotes: Built during an intense 24-hour hackathon. Pyramid CDC focuses on closing the digital divide in underserved Ohio communities.

title: M.C. Jeter's Website

achievements: Faster load times with a revamped design
Estimated 25% uplift in sales and social media interactions
additionalNotes: Early experience with static site generation and user experience improvement. New iteration planned using the lightweight CMS.

Facilitated a seamless macOS experience on non-Apple hardware.
